display.purpose: Hero h1 and article h1 only. Holds the page promise.

headline.purpose: Section header commands ($ ls -la, $ neofetch). Set in Kernel Green so they read as terminal commands, not prose.

title.purpose: Card titles, sub-section h2, featured-card h2.

body.purpose: Paragraph copy in long-form posts and descriptive UI text. Long-form line height lifts to 1.8.

label.purpose: Buttons, nav, tags, badges, footer copyright. The most-used scale on the site.

shadows.name: hover-glow
shadows.purpose: Green-tinted ambient halo on interactive cards (project card, blog post card) under :hover. Paired with translateY(-4px) and a green-tinted border. The only shadow in the system; reserved for state, never structure.

motion.name: ease-quick
motion.purpose: Default duration/ease for button hover, link colour shifts, simple state transitions.

motion.name: ease-card
motion.purpose: Card hover lift, image scale, border tint. Slower than ease-quick to read as a deliberate response.

motion.name: ease-skill
motion.purpose: Skill bar width fill on first scroll-into-view. Heavy ease-out so the bar lands rather than coasts.

motion.name: fade-in-up
motion.purpose: Sectional reveal as elements scroll into view. Translate from +20px Y to 0 with opacity fade. Stagger via 0/0.15s/0.3s delays. Bypassed entirely under prefers-reduced-motion.

motion.name: blink
motion.purpose: Terminal cursor block. Step-end timing keeps the blink crisp, never smooth.

motion.name: ping
motion.purpose: Status indicator halo. The site's only ambient motion at rest. Renders as an expanding green ring at 75% opacity fading to 0.

motion.name: type-reveal
motion.purpose: Terminal line reveal. Steps the clip-path from inset(0 100% 0 0) to inset(0) so characters appear one at a time, like a real shell.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Main call-to-action. Kernel Green surface, Console Black text, leading terminal icon. Coverage budget: at most one per visible viewport.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; padding: 12px 24px; background: #22C55E; color: #0C0C0C; border: none; border-radius: 4px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 500; cursor: pointer; transition: filter 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), transform 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary svg { width: 16px; height: 16px; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { filter: brightness(1.1); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.95); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(34,197,94,0.6); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Quieter companion to Primary. Tier-One Surface with a 1px Subtle Rule border; border darkens slightly on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; gap: 8px; padding: 12px 24px; background: #171717; color: #858585; border: 1px solid #1F1F1F; border-radius: 4px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 13px; font-weight: 400; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-secondary svg { width: 16px; height: 16px; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { border-color: #252525; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(34,197,94,0.6); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: No-chrome variant. Used for tertiary actions where Secondary would be too loud.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 12px 24px; background: transparent; color: #898989; border: none; border-radius: 4px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 13px; cursor: pointer; transition: color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-ghost svg { width: 16px; height: 16px; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { color: #858585; } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid rgba(34,197,94,0.6); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Tag
components.description: Read-only metadata pill. Used for project tech tags and post topics. No interaction.
components.css: .ds-tag { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 4px 10px; background: #1A1A1A; color: #A3A3A3; border-radius: 2px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; line-height: 1.4; }

components.name: Status Indicator
components.description: Pulsing green dot with label. Sits in the header, signalling presence. The site's only ambient motion at rest.
components.css: .ds-status { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; color: #22C55E; } .ds-status-dot { position: relative; display: inline-flex; width: 8px; height: 8px; } .ds-status-pulse { position: absolute; inset: 0; background: #22C55E; border-radius: 9999px; opacity: 0.75; animation: ds-status-ping 1s cubic-bezier(0,0,0.2,1) infinite; } .ds-status-core { position: relative; width: 8px; height: 8px; background: #22C55E; border-radius: 9999px; } @keyframes ds-status-ping { 75%, 100% { transform: scale(2); opacity: 0; } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-status-pulse { animation: none; } }

components.name: Nav Item (Active)
components.description: Header navigation entry. Active state shows a leading green > prompt and a Tier-Two Inset background pill. Inactive entries hold a non-breaking space in place of the prompt to preserve alignment.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 10px 12px; border-radius: 4px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 13px; text-decoration: none; transition: color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); color: #858585; } .ds-nav-item:hover { color: #A3A3A3; } .ds-nav-item-active { background: #1A1A1A; color: #22C55E; font-weight: 500; } .ds-nav-prompt { color: #22C55E; } .ds-nav-item:not(.ds-nav-item-active) .ds-nav-prompt { color: #898989; }

components.name: Card Surface
components.description: Default content card. Tier-One Surface with a 1px Subtle Rule border. Interactive variants (project, blog post) lift on hover with a green-tinted glow; the unstyled default holds still.
components.html:
cashmunk

// privacy-first envelope budgeting app for ios. your data stays on your device, pay once, own forever.

swiftiosprivacy
view_repo →
components.css: .ds-card-interactive { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 12px; background: #171717; border: 1px solid #1F1F1F; border-radius: 4px; padding: 20px; text-decoration: none; transition: transform 300ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), border-color 300ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), box-shadow 300ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-card-interactive:hover { transform: translateY(-4px); border-color: rgba(34,197,94,0.2); box-shadow: 0 10px 15px -3px rgba(34,197,94,0.05), 0 4px 6px -4px rgba(34,197,94,0.05); } .ds-card-title { color: #E5E5E5; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 600; margin: 0; } .ds-card-desc { color: #858585; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 12px; line-height: 1.6; margin: 0; } .ds-card-tags { display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px; } .ds-card-foot { margin-top: auto; display: flex; justify-content: flex-end; padding-top: 4px; } .ds-card-link { color: #858585; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 500; transition: color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-card-interactive:hover .ds-card-link { color: #A3A3A3; } .ds-tag { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 4px 10px; background: #1A1A1A; color: #A3A3A3; border-radius: 2px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 11px; }

components.name: Input Field
components.description: Labelled text field with a leading terminal icon. Tier-Two Inset surface, Subtle Rule border. Focus shows a 2px Kernel Green inset ring at 40% alpha; the input keeps outline-none so the visible state lives on the bordered shell.
components.css: .ds-input-group { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; } .ds-input-label { color: #A3A3A3; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; } .ds-input-shell { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 10px 14px; background: #1A1A1A; border: 1px solid #1F1F1F; border-radius: 4px; transition: border-color 200ms cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-input-shell:focus-within { border-color: rgba(34,197,94,0.5); box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(34,197,94,0.2) inset; } .ds-input-shell svg { width: 14px; height: 14px; color: #898989; flex-shrink: 0; } .ds-input { flex: 1; background: transparent; border: none; outline: none; color: #E5E5E5; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 13px; } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #898989; }

components.name: Section Header
components.description: Top-of-section heading written as a terminal command. The h2 is set in Kernel Green; the description below leads with // as a code comment. Treated as the canonical way to open every landing-page section.
components.html:
$ ls -la ~/projects

// side projects & open source
components.css: .ds-section-header { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 8px; } .ds-section-cmd { margin: 0; color: #22C55E; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 28px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 1.3; } .ds-section-desc { margin: 0; color: #858585; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 13px; } @media (max-width: 767px) { .ds-section-cmd { font-size: 18px; } }

components.name: Terminal Window
components.description: Signature component. Faux-macOS terminal frame holding hero copy, contact details, or any narrative content the site wants to anchor in CLI grammar. Use sparingly — its weight depends on rarity.
components.html:
~/friquelme.dev — bash

$ whoami

florian riquelme — senior software engineer

$ cat intro.txt

9+ years shipping saas platforms, e-commerce, and web apps.

$
components.css: .ds-terminal { width: 100%; overflow: hidden; background: #171717; border: 1px solid #1F1F1F; border-radius: 4px; } .ds-terminal-bar { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; padding: 12px 16px; background: #1A1A1A; border-bottom: 1px solid #1F1F1F; } .ds-terminal-dot { width: 12px; height: 12px; border-radius: 9999px; } .ds-terminal-title { margin-left: 8px; color: #898989; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 12px; } .ds-terminal-body { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 6px; padding: 16px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono Variable', ui-monospace, monospace; font-size: 13px; } @media (min-width: 768px) { .ds-terminal-body { padding: 24px; } } .ds-terminal-cmd { margin: 0; color: #22C55E; font-weight: 500; } .ds-terminal-out { margin: 0; color: #E5E5E5; } .ds-terminal-out-soft { margin: 0; color: #A3A3A3; } .ds-terminal-cursor-row { display: flex; align-items: center; } .ds-terminal-cursor { width: 9px; height: 18px; background: #22C55E; animation: ds-blink 1s steps(1) infinite; } @keyframes ds-blink { 0%, 100% { opacity: 1; } 50% { opacity: 0; } } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-terminal-cursor { animation: none; } }

narrative.overview:
A console for the people who already live in a console. The visual language is the practitioner's workspace, not a depiction of one. Every element ought to feel as if it were rendered by ls, cat, or ps — typeface, prompts, status pulses, comment slashes — and not as nostalgic costume but as the reader's actual habitat. The audience (hiring engineers and EMs at AI-enabled companies) recognises the form on sight; that recognition is the filter, and the filter is the point.

The philosophy is restraint as authority. Where the category shouts (gradients, neon, particle backgrounds, hero-metric templates), this site says less. One accent, one font family, three depth tiers. The site itself is the proof of craft for the brownfield AI thesis: if the portfolio cannot ship with the same discipline that's claimed about production AI, the position collapses. Every visual choice serves the position or gets cut.

Anti-references, carried over from PRODUCT.md and enforced visually here: gradient-heavy SaaS landing pages, neon-on-black 'AI engineer' portfolios, glassmorphism, hero-metric templates, identical card grids, and bootcamp-grad portfolios with decorative SVG illustrations and pastel palettes.
keyCharacteristics: Three-tier tonal depth on a near-black canvas, no shadows at rest
Single saturated accent (Kernel Green) used sparingly for signal, never decoration
Terminal grammar treated as first-class typography: $ prompts, // comments, ~ paths
Lowercase by default in copy and UI; uppercase reserved for nothing
Tight component shapes (4px corners, full borders, no side stripes)

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Kernel Green is the only saturated colour on most screens. If the screen has more than one saturated colour, one of them is wrong. The amber/red/blue semantic trio exists for state, not for decoration; they appear as 8% backgrounds with full-strength text, never as primary surface treatments.

rules.name: The Tonal Ladder Rule
rules.body: Depth is tonal, never shadowed. The three steps are Console Black → Tier-One Surface → Tier-Two Inset. A new component must pick one step, not invent a fourth.

rules.name: The Twin-Gray Rule
rules.body: Comment Gray (#858585) and Decoration Gray (#898989) are intentionally near-identical. Pick by role, not by appearance: Comment Gray for inline content (descriptions, captions, table cells), Decoration Gray for chrome (timestamps, footers, label tier). Never introduce a third gray in this band.

rules.name: The Lowercase Default Rule
rules.body: UI copy and headings default to lowercase. The terminal hates Shift. Capitalisation, when it appears, is in long-form blog body text only — and even there, sentence case dominates.

rules.name: The Comment-as-Caption Rule
rules.body: Descriptive text under a heading or beside a section is framed with leading // . Section descriptions, project blurbs, and footer copyright all follow this. It treats helper text as code commentary, not marketing copy.

rules.name: The Prompt-as-Heading Rule
rules.body: Top-level section headers on landing pages are written as terminal commands prefixed with $ and coloured Kernel Green. They are headings, not decoration; screen readers receive them as h2 elements.

rules.name: The Flat-by-Default Rule
rules.body: No component has a resting shadow. If a designer reaches for box-shadow on a default state, the answer is to step the surface up the tonal ladder instead.

rules.name: The Glow-Means-Interactive Rule
rules.body: The green-tinted hover halo is reserved for elements that change route on click. Static cards (skill bars, status indicators, dividers) do not get the treatment. Hover effects are a state contract, not decoration.

dos: Do keep Kernel Green coverage at or below 10% of any given screen. Its rarity is the meaning.
Do use the three-tier tonal ladder (Console Black → Tier-One Surface → Tier-Two Inset) as the only depth grammar. New components pick a step, not a shadow.
Do treat $ prefixes, // comments, and ~ paths as first-class typography. They are headings and captions, not decoration.
Do write UI copy in lowercase by default. Sentence case appears only in long-form blog body.
Do pair the Kernel Green accent with a non-colour cue (text, icon, prompt prefix) so state never depends on hue alone.

Do hold body text contrast ≥ 4.5:1 and large text / UI components ≥ 3:1 against the dark surfaces. Stdout White on Console Black clears this comfortably; Comment Gray and Decoration Gray sit at the edge — verify before promoting either to body copy.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion. Reduced-motion users get the same content, just without movement.
Do ship hover/focus/active states on every interactive element. A static default-only component reads as dead.
donts: Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a coloured accent stripe on cards, list items, callouts, or alerts. The Callout and prose blockquote use full-perimeter 1px green/20 borders with a 6% green background tint and a leading > glyph — copy that pattern for any new emphasised block.
Don't write gradient text. No background-clip: text with a gradient. Single solid colour, emphasis via weight or size.
Don't use glassmorphism. No backdrop blurs as decoration. The dark surfaces don't need fog.
Don't introduce a hero-metric template (big number, small label, supporting stats, gradient accent). The MetricCard primitive exists for genuine data display, not for SaaS-cliché identity.

Don't ship identical card grids. The homepage already varies card sizes (project grid 2-up, blog grid 3-up, featured card full-width). Maintain that variation; do not collapse to a single card system.
Don't reach for a modal as the first thought. Inline disclosure, progressive enhancement, or a separate route almost always beats a modal on this site.
Don't introduce a third gray near #858585 / #898989. The pair already covers tertiary and chrome roles. New muted needs pick one; they don't add a third.
Don't add a second display font, body font, or sans companion. JetBrains Mono carries every role.
Don't wrap copy in a max-w-prose container reflexively. Most landing-page sections want their own measure.
Don't write em dashes in copy. Use commas, colons, semicolons, periods, or parentheses. This rule applies in .astro and .mdx files alike.
Don't use #000 or #fff anywhere. Console Black (#0C0C0C) and Stdout White (#E5E5E5) are the only legal extremes.
Don't describe yourself with adjectives the audience can't verify ('AI-driven', 'scalable', 'robust'). Show, don't tell.

Don't ship a marketing aesthetic. Neon, particles, animated gradients, 'Trusted by' logo bars, and gradient-heavy CTA bands are the anti-position. If the screen could appear unchanged on a generic AI-product landing page, redo it.
